payload.event_name: Joint MTA Board and Committees Meeting
payload.description: Joint meeting of TBTA, Joint Railroad, New York City Transit, Capital Program, Audit and Finance Committees and the MTA Board

warnings: event.date is accepted as passthrough, but prefer date_text. Date literal.

payload.scope: NYCT subway, bus and paratransit combined

payload.raw_value_text: overall subway crime down 14% compared to 2019

payload.claim_text: For 2025, MTA Audit completed 80 operational, financial and technology audits and identified $11.5 million in potential savings and cost efficiencies. The contract group completed 830 contract reviews covering $165 million in audited costs of which $11.9 million were identified as questioned costs for which we typically see a 90% recovery rate.
payload.description: Summary of 2025 audit results from Auditor General Monica Murray

payload.comparison: more than double the agency's five-year average since 2020

comparison_normalized.raw_text: more than double the agency's five-year average since 2020

payload.raw_value_text: $2.2 billion total support to MTA mass transit including CRZ

payload.claim_text: MTA will take a serious look into the possibility of keeping the tunnel boring machine moving when it reaches the end of its path in this phase of the Second Avenue Subway. MTA will look to see if it may be continued west, perhaps all the way to Broadway to lay the core groundwork for the next phase of Second Avenue Subway. MTA is not committing financially to a project however MTA will explore it and the opportunity to explain what MTA is calling the Q West project, Q to the west – across 125th Street.
payload.description: Chair Lieber announced exploration of extending Second Avenue Subway tunnel boring machine west across 125th Street to Broadway

payload.claim_text: A study will be conducted jointly with the Port Authority for a much better Jamaica Station. The station is the fourth largest train station, in terms of volume, in North America.
payload.description: Governor Hochul proposed improving Jamaica Station; joint study with Port Authority

payload.claim_text: MTA IT developed a solution to support fare evasion enforcement on Buses that replaces handwritten Notice Of Violations with an iOS app for issuance, Bluetooth printing, and back-end integrations into a centralized ticket management database. Implementation includes handheld device to fully digitize ticket issuance, enabled real citation issuance/ticket printing, and will enable real time recidivism lookup.
payload.description: Technology project for fare evasion enforcement on buses

payload.claim_text: MTA IT updated and standardized how MNR and LIRR sell tickets, replaced old ticket machines, reduced credit-card security requirements, and built a modern technology ecosystem. Consolidated into a single application for MNR & LIRR, single IT team for both railroads, migrated from On-Premise servers to AWS Cloud Architecture, introduced cross-selling of LIRR & MNR ticket products, and introduced QR Codes on paper ticket products.
payload.description: Technology project for LIRR and Metro-North fare payment modernization

payload.claim_text: MTA IT replaced old handheld ticket machines with modern Apple iPhone devices and moved credit-card processing to a new provider. The new OSVD system runs one shared app for onboard ticket sales, validation, and receipts. Achievements include EMV chip/contactless credit/debit including Apple Pay & Google Pay, and replaced non-supported gateway for credit/debit processing.
payload.description: Technology project replacing conductor handheld ticket machines with iPhones

payload.claim_text: MTA IT developed a solution to enhance ridership experience across subways and buses. Features include zero-tap information, nearby arrivals, personalized map with live bus locations, Spanish language support, station modeling, transfers, and screen reader on iOS and Android.
payload.description: New MTA Transit App for subways and buses

payload.event_name: Deloitte 2025 MTA Financial Statement Audit Report
payload.description: Deloitte & Touche LLP issued unmodified opinion on MTA's 2025 consolidated financial statements

warnings: event.date is accepted as passthrough, but prefer date_text. Date literal.

tool_args.label: Metro-North Super Express Train Time Savings

payload.raw_value_text: saving riders up to 25 minutes per trip
payload.scope: Metro-North Super Express Train on Hudson and New Haven Lines
TBTA Intelligent Transportation Systems and Central Maintenance Divisions

payload.claim_text: MTA IT transitioned its technology model to align directly with the business units, transforming solution delivery to be led by internal tech resources. New model has effectively positioned MTA IT to develop and deploy business-supporting applications faster, more reliable and in direct partnerships with MTA HQ departments and operating agencies. Reduced reliance on external consultants. Reduced vacancy rate from 15-18% to rolling 5-6%.
payload.description: CTO Raf Portnoy's Technology Delivery Transformation update

tool_args.label: New Fare and Ticketing Customer Savings
Auditor General Reports to Audit Committee

payload.gap_text: Document has 642 pages with 6919 blocks. Only key narrative pages and financial statement summaries were extracted and submitted. Detailed agency-level financial statements (NYC Transit pp180-270, LIRR pp271-344, Metro-North pp345-413, MTA Bus pp414-471, TBTA pp472-548, SIRTOA pp549-604, FMTAC pp605-628), work plan details, and the remediation plans monitoring report were not fully ingested.
